text.title: C# .NET Core Backend Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the .NET Core service umbrella. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Every async controller action and service method MUST accept `CancellationToken` as the last parameter and thread it to every downstream IO call (EF, HttpClient, channel reads).

rationale.source: Microsoft async guidelines + internal load-test postmortems

rule.rationale: Without token plumbing, client disconnects and server timeouts do not cancel database queries, causing thread-pool starvation under load.

rule.statement: Register `DbContext` as Scoped, `IHttpClientFactory`-backed clients as Transient, hosted services as Singleton. Resolve scoped dependencies inside a `BackgroundService` via `IServiceScopeFactory.CreateScope()` — never via constructor injection.
rule.rationale: BackgroundService is singleton; injecting a scoped DbContext into its constructor throws at runtime on first use (or worse: silently shares state across iterations).

rule.statement: Forbid `.Result`, `.Wait()`, `GetAwaiter().GetResult()`, and `async void` (except for top-level event handlers). Reviewers MUST reject diffs that introduce any of these.
rule.rationale: Sync-over-async deadlocks under a synchronisation context; `async void` swallows exceptions and crashes the host.

rule.statement: EF Core read paths MUST call `AsNoTracking()`. Bulk operations MUST use `ExecuteUpdateAsync`/`ExecuteDeleteAsync`. Repositories MUST NOT return `IQueryable<T>` — materialize with `ToListAsync`/`ToArrayAsync` before crossing the layer boundary.
rule.rationale: Change tracker bloats memory on read-only paths; leaked `IQueryable` lets controllers execute queries against a disposed context.

rule.statement: Error responses MUST conform to RFC 7807 ProblemDetails. Register `AddProblemDetails()` in `Program.cs`; never return ad-hoc anonymous error envelopes from controllers.
rule.rationale: A single error contract lets clients (mobile, BFF, partners) implement one parser; ad-hoc envelopes multiply parsing logic.

rule.statement: Source MUST be organised by feature (`Features/Users/{UsersController.cs, UserService.cs, UserDtos.cs, UserMappings.cs}`), not by technical layer (`Controllers/`, `Services/`, `Dtos/`).
rule.rationale: Feature folders keep cohesive changes in one directory; technical-layer trees scatter a single feature across five folders and hide the surface area from the agent.
